terms.supervisor: Control plane. Owns persistent state, space registry, plugin/service discovery, catalog generation, service readiness, runtime launch, and process supervision.
terms.runtime: Execution engine. Owns sessions, prompt assembly, LLM/tool loop, service function dispatch through tool calls, events, and agent profile execution.
terms.agent_plugin: Installable definition of an agent role, prompts, skills, default permissions, handoff rules, and evaluation requirements.
terms.agent_profile: Resolved runtime contract produced from an agent plugin plus Quarkfile/session overrides.
terms.service_plugin: Installable descriptor for a gRPC service and its exported service functions.
terms.service_function: Agent-facing callable service capability exposed through the runtime tool-call envelope.
terms.tool_plugin: Installable tool implementation with schema, execution code, and SKILL.md guidance.
terms.provider_plugin: Legacy model-provider adapter category removed from runtime; provider dispatch is owned by Gateway.
terms.space: Isolated workspace identity and persistent state boundary.
terms.catalog: Supervisor-resolved startup input consumed by runtime.
terms.quarkfile_override: Space-level configuration overlay that supervisor validates before runtime launch.

owns: service function dispatch from resolved catalog
Quarkfile read/write in current working directory
direct runtime client calls after supervisor resolution
service-local persistence where the service owns the durable domain

runtime: split prompt, session, inference, service function dispatch, handoff, and event ownership
prevent fallback runtime discovery when supervisor catalog is present
supervisor: split HTTP handlers, runtime lifecycle, registry mutation, launch environment, and catalog generation
move environment assembly out of runtime launcher
services_and_plugins: separate transport, domain, persistence, config, and service function description ownership
keep services from calling each other
e2e: separate process lifecycle, fixtures, prompts, assertions, artifacts, and external dependencies

known_import_exceptions.reason: Runtime still imports supervisor client directly for space-scoped data operations. This should move behind a shared client contract or runtime boundary adapter.
Supervisor embeds the space service directly during bootstrap. This needs a cleaner service lifecycle boundary when supervisor runtime lifecycle and Core services are split.
